title: CLAUDE.md
text: file provides guidance Claude Code (claude.ai/code) working code repository.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: mori — Shared Memory R Objects. Uses POSIX shared memory (Linux, macOS) Win32 file mappings (Windows) R’s ALTREP framework let multiple processes machine read physical memory pages. external dependencies. Requires R >= 4.3.0 (ALTLIST). API: share() → ALTREP shared object, map_shared() → open SHM name, shared_name() → extract identifier, is_shared() → test shared, prune_shared() → remove orphaned regions dead processes. ALTREP serialization hooks emit shared_name() identifier wire form — transparent serialize() mirai.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Standard R-package workflow (devtools::test(), devtools::document(), R CMD build / R CMD check). Run single test file testthat::test_file("tests/testthat/test-nested.R").

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Zero-copy (SHM-backed): atomic vectors (type, attributes) data frame columns written directly SHM backed ALTREP consumers. Attributes serialize trailing region reapplied consumer. Numeric Dataptr_or_null returns SHM pointer; Dataptr(writable=TRUE) triggers COW private copy. String Elt lazily creates CHARSXPs; Dataptr_or_null returns NULL force per-element access. Nested lists (zero-copy): VECSXP/LISTSXP elements shared list written inline complete child MORL region data_offset; level wraps ALTLIST. is_shared() returns TRUE sub-lists; shared_name() returns path-bearing identifier; map_shared(shared_name(sub)) opens addressed sub-list / element directly. OS-level region name prefix [ recoverable via sub("\\[.*$", "", shared_name(x)). Pass-: R objects (environments, closures, language objects, NULL) returned unchanged. SHM created.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: mori write-host, read-many consumers, COW mutation. share() allocates new SHM region unique name; existing regions never mutated. name embeds creator PID (mori_<pid>_<counter>); mori_shm_create opens O_EXCL (POSIX) / checks ERROR_ALREADY_EXISTS (Windows); name already exists — orphan left crashed -PID process — surfaced error (MORI_EEXIST), never worked around reusing mutating existing region. prune_shared() mechanism clearing orphans; must run PID free (reuse), since colliding process reads PID alive reap . host writes full region inside mori_create returning, consumers obtain name call returns — partial writes observable. Consumer mappings read-(PROT_READ / FILE_MAP_READ); mutations trigger COW private memory. locking implemented required; future change admitting -place mutation breaks model.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: R exported functions single .Call wrappers. mori_create dispatches TYPEOF(x): Already mori-backed ALTREP (mori_owned_tag R_altrep_data1(x)) → return x unchanged. Idempotence required: re-sharing sub-list view must allocate fresh root region (break shared_name()). NILSXP → returned -. VECSXP/LISTSXP → mori_shm_create_list_call — ALTLIST per-element directory. Pairlists coerced VECSXP every level. Data frames go path. STRSXP → mori_shm_create_string_call — ALTSTRING offset table + packed strings. SHM-eligible atomics (REALSXP, INTSXP, LGLSXP, RAWSXP, CPLXSXP) → mori_shm_create_vector_call. Everything else → returned -. path returns via mori_make_result, chains host extptr ALTREP’s keeper chain (see Internal State Lifetime).

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Writing SHM count--write two flavours: mori_serialize_count / mori_serialize_into (serialize.c, fallback serialized bytes) recursive mori_nested_size / mori_nested_write (altrep.c, whole MORL regions). count writer disagree even one byte, SHM region malformed consumers read garbage. change either side must mirrored .

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Registered mori_altrep_init. Numeric types — mori_real, mori_integer, mori_logical, mori_raw, mori_complex — share mori_vec pattern SHM-backed data pointer. mori_string uses mori_str lazy per-element access via Elt Rf_mkCharLenCE. mori_list uses mori_list_view per-element directory lazy Elt cache uses R_NilValue “uncached” sentinel — fresh VECSXP naturally R_NilValue-filled, cache init zero work. NIL-valued elements sole singleton output mori_unwrap_element’s fallback path, aren’t cached; re-extraction returns R_NilValue identity preserved free.

what: COW invariant for new ALTREP methods
title: CLAUDE.md
text: method returning writable pointer (mutating) must check R_altrep_data2(x) first — non-null, vector materialized private copy SHM pointer longer authoritative. New Dataptr / coercion methods routinely forget . Dataptr_or_null returns materialized copy data2 set, SHM pointer otherwise.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: classes register Serialized_state Unserialize. Identifier wire form (single canonical shape). mori_format_chain single source truth used mori_shm_name (.Call) three Serialized_state methods. Walks keeper chain mori_owned_tag hops collecting view->index (>= 0 ) formats <prefix> (root) <prefix>[i1+1,...] (sub-object) caller-provided stack buffer. shm->name_len cached create/open time (per-call strlen). 1-based externalisation cues R’s [[]] semantics; parser converts back 0-based. Unserialize. mori_Unserialize delegates STRSXP × 1 state mori_shm_open_and_wrap; parse-success returns opened ALTREP, parse-failure falls expanded-state handling (COW-materialized ALTSTRING case). mori_shm_open_and_wrap runs mori_parse_identifier: prefix-routes magic-dispatch; path form routes mori_open_path_c, walks intermediate VECSXP directory via mori_make_view_extptr (bare keeper-chain extptr — ALTLIST wrapper, attribute restoration, since intermediates never observed) calls mori_unwrap_element leaf. Fallback full materialization : - COW-materialized vectors (data2 set) — returns materialized copy. - Nesting depth exceeds MORI_MAX_PATH (64) mori_format_chain.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: MORI_PREFIX_LITERAL (mori.h) /mori_ (POSIX) / Local\\mori_ (Windows) single source truth shared shm.c’s name-format strings (MORI_PREFIX_LITERAL "%x_%x" POSIX, MORI_PREFIX_LITERAL "%lx_%x" Windows) parser’s literal memcmp. Change ; sides stay sync. Names variable-width hex (zero-padding); MORI_NAME_MAX = 30 bounds prefix either platform (Windows worst case: Local\\mori_<8hex>_<8hex> = 28 chars + NUL). mori_parse_identifier bounded single-pass (length-capped MORI_IDENTIFIER_MAX); indices externalised 1-based stored 0-based. path-parse loop relies NUL-termination sentinel (every fail-branch’s predicate excludes \0), trailing p < eos checks aren’t needed . Sizing invariant: MORI_NAME_MAX + 2 + 11 × MORI_MAX_PATH < MORI_FORMAT_BUFLEN.
code: identifier ::= prefix [ "[" int1 ("," int1)* "]" ] prefix     ::= MORI_PREFIX_LITERAL hex+ "_" hex+ hex+       ::= [0-9a-f]+         # one or more, lowercase int1       ::= [1-9][0-9]*       # 1-based, no leading zeros

title: CLAUDE.md
text: map_shared unserialize share mori_parse_identifier shape validation differ failure mode: malformed input → NULL (wrong type/length, NA, missing malformed prefix, malformed bracketed path); well-formed identifier fails map → error (missing region, bad magic, truncated header, OOB path index, non-VECSXP intermediate). Preserve split mori_shm_open_and_wrap: collapsing either way breaks probe-vs-corruption distinction.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Magic identifier first 4 bytes: MORH (0x4D4F5248) atomic vector, MORL (0x4D4F524C) ALTLIST, MORS (0x4D4F5253) ALTSTRING. tables canonical spec; mori_serialize_into mori_nested_write implementations. MORH — atomic vector. 64-byte header, data starts byte 64 (64-byte aligned SIMD). Trailing serialized attributes () follow data. Attributes serialized R object (pairlist R < 4.6, named list otherwise); mori_restore_attrs unserializes reapplies consumer. MORL — ALTLIST. Header + element directory + per-element data regions. directory entry (32 bytes): data_offset(8) + data_size(8) + sexptype(4) + attrs_size(4) + length(8). sexptype semantics: 0 → serialized bytes (via serialize.c); STRSXP → offset table + packed strings data_offset; VECSXP → nested MORL region inlined data_offset size data_size (child’s header / directory / elements / attrs inline; parent’s attrs_size always 0 VECSXP children); → raw zero-copy data. non-VECSXP entries attrs_size > 0, attrs appended within data region data_offset + data_size - attrs_size. MORS — ALTSTRING. 24-byte header + offset table + packed string bytes + optional trailing attributes. offset table entry (16 bytes): str_offset(int64) + str_length(int32) + str_encoding(int32). str_offset relative start packed string area. str_length < 0 means NA_STRING. str_encoding cetype_t (0=native, 1=UTF-8, 2=Latin-1, 3=bytes). layout used inline STRSXP elements within ALTLIST regions (offset table starts element’s data_offset; element-level attrs use directory entry’s attrs_size field).

title: CLAUDE.md
text: SHM lifetime fully automatic via chained external pointer finalizers. Three interned symbols (C globals altrep.c): mori_shm_tag (Rf_install("mori_shm")) — tags SHM mapping extptr (host- consumer-side). Addr mori_shm *; finalizer: munmap. mori_host_tag (Rf_install("mori_host")) — tags host-unlink extptr created mori_make_result. Addr mori_shm * carrying name (POSIX) HANDLE (Windows); finalizer: shm_unlink / CloseHandle. Sits shm terminus keeper chain. mori_owned_tag (Rf_install("mori_owned")) — tags every malloc-backed extptr keeper chain: mori ALTREP’s data1 extptr (view, vec, str) bare keeper-chain extptrs created mori_make_view_extptr path-walk intermediates (ALTREP wrapper still hold mori_list_view *). Addr type ALTREP data1 recovered TYPEOF(x) ALTREP boundary: VECSXP → mori_list_view *, STRSXP → mori_str *, else mori_vec *. Bare keeper-chain hops always mori_list_view * (vec/str ever appear leaves). Standalone-vs-element carried struct’s index field (-1 standalone/root, >= 0 element/sub-list); redundantly recoverable data1.prot’s tag (shm_tag → standalone, owned_tag → element/sub-list). Common finalizer: mori_owned_finalizer frees addr. is_shared() pointer comparison mori_owned_tag. mori_shm_name() invokes mori_format_chain emit bare prefix roots / standalones path-bearing form (<prefix>[i1,i2,...], 1-based) sub-objects.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Host side (share): mori_shm_create allocates mmaps region; POSIX fd closed mmap (mapping stays valid). mori_make_result splits ownership — ALTREP wrapper gets mapping via mori_shm_tag extptr (finalizer → munmap); host extptr (mori_host_tag, finalizer → shm_unlink / CloseHandle) chained extptr’s prot. finalizers run ALTREP GC’d. R_RegisterCFinalizerEx(..., TRUE) covers session exit. Consumer side (map_shared / unserialize): mori_shm_open maps read-(size via fstat / VirtualQuery); POSIX fd closed mmap (Windows: handle retained UnmapViewOfFile). shm extptr’s finalizer munmap — never unlink. lists opened prefix, shm extptr’s immediate child root view extptr (mori_list_view *, index=-1) becomes ALTREP data1; sub-lists accessed via Elt (index >= 0) chain parent view extptr way. path-form map_shared("/mori_x_y[,j,...]"), intermediate hops bare keeper-chain extptrs (mori_make_view_extptr, ALTLIST) — leaf’s ALTREP exists. chain shape identical mori_format_chain’s purposes either way. Element vectors hold mori_vec / mori_str extptr whose prot immediate parent view (shm extptr vec/str root). Element lifetime: leaves keep root SHM alive chain (leaf → parent view(s) → shm extptr → host extptr). Every R_MakeExternalPtr retains prot, chain survives even enclosing ALTLIST(s) GC’d. mori_format_chain chain walker: walks mori_owned_tag hops (always mori_list_view * — vec/str extptrs ever leaves), collecting view indices (skipping root view’s -1) hits shm extptr.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: mori.h — types (mori_shm, mori_buf, mori_vec index field, mori_list_view), declarations, MORI_ALIGN64, mori_sizeof_elt, identifier grammar constants (MORI_NAME_MAX, MORI_MAX_PATH, MORI_IDENTIFIER_MAX, MORI_FORMAT_BUFLEN, MORI_PREFIX_LITERAL). shm.c — platform SHM create/open/close + finalizers sides. mori_shm_reap enumerates platform’s name source (#ifdef-selected) — /dev/shm Linux (entries region names), per-user registry dir macOS (per-process logs; see Platform Notes) — classifies PID embedded name via mori_pid_alive (Linux unlinks dead-PID region directly; macOS reads dead-PID log via mori_reap_log unlinks regions names), feeds shared per-name core mori_reap_unlink (shm_unlink + record--reclaim). Windows / POSIX reap → NULL / *n == 0. mori_shm_os_unlink single region-unlink seam (host finalizer, create-fail, reap) pure shm_unlink; macOS registry decoupled managed mori_log_append / mori_log_release (see Platform Notes). Reap semantics , platforms: dead-PID region reported removed shm_unlink actually reclaimed , concurrent reaps never double-report. Error path: mori_err_classify (static; native errno / GetLastError → portable MORI_E* category) mori_err_describe (category summary + platform remediation hint); mori_shm_create / mori_shm_create_heap return MORI_OK failure category, classifying native code close / unlink / CloseHandle clobbers errno / GetLastError. serialize.c — mori_serialize_count, mori_serialize_into, mori_unserialize_from. Used fallback (non-zero-copy) ALTLIST entries directory sexptype == 0. altrep.c — ALTREP class definitions, mori_create dispatcher, recursive writers (mori_nested_size / mori_nested_write), consumer open+wrap (mori_shm_open_and_wrap), path open (mori_open_path_c), view constructors (mori_make_view_extptr bare keeper-chain extptrs, mori_make_list_view full ALTLIST + attrs), identity/name (mori_is_shared / mori_shm_name), identifier formatter / parser (mori_format_chain / mori_parse_identifier), serialization hooks, mori_altrep_init. create-failure message lives mori_shm_create_failed, composes requested size (mori_format_bytes) mori_err_describe’s summary + hint single Rf_error. init.c — R_init_mori, .Call registration table (5 entries: mori_create, mori_shm_open_and_wrap, mori_is_shared, mori_shm_name, mori_prune). .Call names match C function names; entry points take single SEXP except mori_prune, takes none.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: mori-package.R (package docs) share.R (five .Call wrappers — dispatch error handling C level; prune_shared() delegates mori_prune, returns NULL nothing pruned). import-standalone-defer.R vendored copy withr’s defer() (usethis::use_standalone("r-lib/withr", "defer")) used tests — edit hand; refresh via use_standalone.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: testthat edition 3. Entry point tests/testthat.R; tests tests/testthat/test-*.R grouped topic (file’s desc() strings authoritative). tests run unconditionally — nothing gated behind skip_on_cran environment variables.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Linux: SHM /dev/shm/ (tmpfs). Accessed via open() / unlink() directly avoid -lrt link dependency shm_open requires. MAP_POPULATE pre-faults pages. macOS: Kernel-backed SHM via shm_open / shm_unlink (libc, extra link flags). MAP_POPULATE -op (defined 0). kernel POSIX-shm namespace filesystem footprint enumerated (invisible ipcs / lsof creator dies), orphan reaping relies per-user registry TMPDIR (resolved via $TMPDIR, confstr(_CS_DARWIN_USER_TEMP_DIR), /tmp) mori_shm_reap scans place /dev/shm. registry must live enumerable namespace — putting SHM face non-enumerability wall — filesystem-backed, keep per-share() cost critical path one append-log per process (<dir>/mori_<pid>) rather file per region: mori_log_append (called mori_shm_create region observable) opens log lazily first share appends one line per region name — single write() (~1 µs) vs creating file (~40 µs APFS, neither path resolution cached dir fd helps cost inode allocation + journaling). Single-writer per process ⇒ locking; reader reaper dead PID, definition concurrent writer. mori_log_release (host finalizer / create-fail) decrements process-global live-region count , zero, closes+unlinks log rmdirs now-empty dir — dir tracks live-region set per-region markers , driven finalization. count incremented unconditionally stays balanced logging fails (forfeits reapability, never region). Fork-safe: mori_log_fork_guard reopens log getpid() changes (inherited fd parent’s — closed, never unlinked). Crash semantics match regions’: write() visible cross-process via page cache without fsync survives writer’s death, lost reboot. Path resolution (mori_log_dir) pure — builds/caches path never creates dir (mori_log_append retries mkdir ENOENT sole creator), reap process log opens nothing leaves nothing behind. log ops best-effort. Windows: Page-file-backed via CreateFileMappingA / MapViewOfFile. kernel32 always available. Host must keep mapping handle alive consumers opened (GC-chained host extptr handles ).

title: CLAUDE.md
text: roxygen2 markdown support; NAMESPACE auto-generated. MIT license. README.md generated README.Rmd — edit .Rmd re-knit, never edit README.md directly. CLAUDE.md .claude/ excluded package builds (via .Rbuildignore).

title: Authors and Citation
text: Gao C (2026). mori: Shared Memory R Objects. R package version 0.2.1.9000, https://shikokuchuo.net/mori/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {mori: Shared Memory for R Objects},   author = {Charlie Gao},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.2.1.9000},   url = {https://shikokuchuo.net/mori/}, }

title: Shared Memory for R Objects
text: Shared Memory R Objects → share() writes R object shared memory returns shared version → Compact ALTREP serialization — shared objects travel transparently serialize() mirai() → Lazy access automatic cleanup — read demand; freed R’s garbage collector → OS-level shared memory (POSIX / Win32) — pure C, external dependencies

title: Shared Memory for R Objects
text: Parallel computing multiplies memory. 8 workers need 200 MB dataset, 1.6 GB serialization, transfer, deserialization — 8 separate copies consuming RAM. share() writes data shared memory worker maps physical pages — turning per-worker copies per-worker references. Without mori, worker holds full data frame. mori, worker holds small reference shared region: Avoiding 8 × 200 MB serialize / deserialize also translates significant runtime saving:
code: library(mori) library(mirai) library(lobstr)  daemons(8)  # 200 MB data frame — 5 columns × 5M rows df <- as.data.frame(matrix(rnorm(25e6), ncol = 5)) shared_df <- share(df) mirai_map(1:8, \(i, data) format(lobstr::obj_size(data)),           .args = list(data = df))[.flat] |> unique() #> [1] "200.00 MB"  mirai_map(1:8, \(i, data) format(lobstr::obj_size(data)),           .args = list(data = shared_df))[.flat] |> unique() #> [1] "824 B" boot_mean <- \(i, data) colMeans(data[sample(nrow(data), replace = TRUE), ])  # Without mori — each daemon deserializes a full copy mirai_map(1:8, boot_mean, .args = list(data = df))[] |> system.time() #>    user  system elapsed  #>   0.636  12.832   8.424  # With mori — each daemon maps the same shared memory mirai_map(1:8, boot_mean, .args = list(data = shared_df))[] |> system.time() #>    user  system elapsed  #>   0.002   0.003   4.787  daemons(0)

title: Shared Memory for R Objects
text: Workers must run machine — mori shares physical RAM, bytes network.

title: Shared Memory for R Objects
text: shared_name() returns shared memory name shared object; map_shared() opens region name — useful handing reference processes without going serialization:
code: x <- share(rnorm(1e6))  shared_name(x) #> [1] "/mori_d04e_1"  # Another process — here the same one — can map the region by name y <- map_shared(shared_name(x)) identical(x[], y[]) #> [1] TRUE

title: Shared Memory for R Objects
text: ALTREP serialization hooks emit identifier wire, serialized form bytes regardless data size: transparent R serialization pathway — mirai, parallel, callr, base R serialize() carry shared objects references rather copies. Sub-elements shared list serialize references — element travels path parent shared region, full data:
code: length(serialize(x, NULL)) #> [1] 124 daemons(3)  # Share a list — all 3 vectors in a single shared region lst <- share(list(a = rnorm(1e6), b = rnorm(1e6), c = rnorm(1e6)))  # Each element arrives on the worker as a zero-copy reference mirai_map(lst, \(v) format(lobstr::obj_size(v)))[.flat] |> unique() #> [1] "904 B"  daemons(0)

title: Shared Memory for R Objects
text: atomic vector types lists / data frames written directly shared memory, attributes preserved end--end. Pairlists coerced lists. share() returns ALTREP wrappers point shared pages — deserialization, per-process memory allocation. R objects (environments, closures, language objects) returned unchanged share() — shared memory region created.

title: Shared Memory for R Objects
text: data frame lives single shared region; columns read demand, worker needs 3 100 columns loads 3. Character strings accessed lazily per element.
code: df <- share(as.data.frame(matrix(rnorm(1e7), ncol = 100))) shared_name(df)        # one region for all 100 columns #> [1] "/mori_d04e_3" shared_name(df[[50]])  # sub-path into the same region #> [1] "/mori_d04e_3[50]"

title: Shared Memory for R Objects
text: Shared memory managed R’s garbage collector. shared memory region stays alive long shared object backed remains referenced R — original returned share(), column sub-list extracted , another process. references remain — session exits cleanly — shared memory freed automatically. Important: Ensure return value share() garbage collected consumer can map shared memory. process killed cleanup can run — crash, SIGKILL, OOM killer — region can left behind. prune_shared() reclaims orphans, removing regions whose creating process longer running.

title: Shared Memory for R Objects
text: Shared data mapped read-, preventing corruption shared region. Mutations always local — R’s copy--write mechanism ensures processes continue reading original shared data: Structural changes list data frame (adding, removing, reordering elements) produce regular R list. shared region unaffected. Modifying values within shared vector (e.g., X[1] <- 0) materializes just vector private copy. vectors shared region stay zero-copy. – Please note mori project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project, agree abide terms.

text: TRUE FALSE.

title: Test if an Object is Shared — is_shared
code: x <- 1:100 y <- share(x) is_shared(y) #> [1] TRUE is_shared(x) #> [1] FALSE

text: name character string returned shared_name(): either bare shared memory name (opens root) name 1-based bracketed index path (e.g. "/mori_abc_1[2,3]", opens addressed sub-list element directly).

title: Open Shared Memory by Name — map_shared
text: R object stored named region (sub-object given path), NULL name valid shared memory name (wrong type, length, NA, missing malformed prefix, malformed bracketed path). name parses valid region absent corrupted — path address valid sub-object — error raised.

title: Open Shared Memory by Name — map_shared

what: mori: Shared Memory for R Objects — mori-package
title: mori: Shared Memory for R Objects — mori-package
text: Share R objects via shared memory share(), access processes map_shared(), using R's ALTREP framework zero-copy memory-mapped access. Shared objects serialize compactly via ALTREP serialization hooks. Shared memory automatically freed R object garbage collected.

what: Prune Orphaned Shared Memory Regions — prune_shared
title: Prune Orphaned Shared Memory Regions — prune_shared
text: Recover shared memory regions leaked process killed clean — example crash, SIGKILL, --memory killer. normally need : mori frees shared memory automatically share() object garbage-collected clean R session exit (see Details). function relevant Linux macOS. title: Prune Orphaned Shared Memory Regions — prune_shared
text: Shared memory normally managed automatically: region unlinked share() object owns garbage-collected, clean R session exit. region left behind owning process killed either can run. prune_shared() removes leftovers. Pruning conservative: region removed creating process (encoded region name) longer running, regions still use live process never touched. Removing region unlinks name; processes already mapped keep reading release , memory freed last mapping gone.

title: Prune Orphaned Shared Memory Regions — prune_shared
code: # Shared memory is freed automatically - you do not normally call this. # To prune regions left behind by a crashed process: prune_shared()

title: Create a Shared Object — share
code: x <- share(1:100) sum(x) #> [1] 5050  lst <- share(list(a = 1:3, b = letters)) is_shared(lst) #> [1] TRUE

title: Extract Shared Memory Name — shared_name
text: character string identifying shared object, NULL x shared object. sub-list element extracted shared list, string carries bracketed 1-based index path (e.g. "/mori_abc_1[2,3]"). map_shared() accepts forms; path-qualified form returns addressed sub-object directly. underlying shared memory region name prefix [ recoverable via sub("\\[.*$", "", shared_name(x)).

title: Extract Shared Memory Name — shared_name
code: x <- share(1:100) shared_name(x) #> [1] "/mori_19cf_5"  # A sub-object extracted from a shared list carries a bracketed index path: lst <- share(list(a = 1:3, b = letters)) shared_name(lst[[2]]) #> [1] "/mori_19cf_6[2]"

title: mori 0.2.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-09 New prune_shared() removes shared memory regions orphaned process killed clean (#25). share() fails create shared memory region now reports requested size , applicable, actionable hint (e.g. raising container’s /dev/shm limit) instead generic message (#29).

title: mori 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-05-09 Wire-format change (breaking): serialization format shared memory region naming scheme changed. shared_name() map_shared() now round-trip sub-lists extracted elements, just whole shared objects. Sub-object identifiers carry bracketed index path (e.g. /mori_abc_1[2,3]). shared_name() now returns NULL non-shared inputs (previously empty string ""); shared_name(x) %||% "" retains previous behaviour. share() Linux fails clean R error /dev/shm small (typical containers, limit can raised start) instead SIGBUS-ing mid-serialize (#14). map_shared() Linux longer prefaults whole region, restoring lazy first-touch access match macOS.

title: mori 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-21 Initial CRAN release.
